D8 AnthropologyL2 UnderstandReference

Virgin's Blue — Ultramarine in the Mantle

Medieval contracts required the Virgin Mary's mantle in ultramarine — the most expensive pigment, more costly than gold leaf; defaulting on quality was a contractual breach

By the 13th century, ultramarine had become Mary's signature colour — partly theology (heaven, divinity, purity) and partly economics (showing patron piety through expense). Renaissance commission contracts specified ultramarine quality, with penalty clauses for substitution. A 1485 contract for Pinturicchio's chapel reserved the highest grade for Mary's mantle.

Pick pigment grade — affects how the Virgin reads under candlelight.
